response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced generalist with nearly two decades of software development spanning web, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems, and comfortably meets the raw years-of-experience and language-proficiency bars. However, the role's core domain — experimentation platforms, A/B testing, feature flighting, telemetry foundations, metrics/guardrail design, and statistical analysis — is entirely absent from the resume. There is also little evidence of principal-level scope: the career history shows strong senior/staff IC contributions to feature teams, but not architecting systemic platforms adopted across multiple teams or leading multi-workstream initiatives, which is the essence of this IC6 position.
strengths: 15+ years of broad engineering experience across languages (TypeScript, C/C++, Java, C#, DLang) with a BS in Computer Science
Real high-scale distributed systems work at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (low-latency services handling millions of HTTP requests per second)
Recent hands-on AI tooling experience (MCP code generators, agentic workflows) plus strong testing culture, open-source work, and mentorship via workshops
concerns: No experimentation, A/B testing, feature-flagging, or growth-measurement experience anywhere in the work history
No demonstrated principal-level leadership: no cross-team platform ownership, multi-workstream initiatives, or org-wide architectural influence
Limited evidence of data/statistics aptitude — no telemetry, metrics infrastructure, or experimental-analysis work described

key_factors.factor: Scope gap between senior IC history and principal-level expectations
Strong general engineering breadth and high-scale systems background
